memoryArtifacts.headline: Sir Richard de Sutton - Knight - Biography
memoryArtifacts.description:
Biography

Sir Richard de SUTTON Knight was born 29 Sep 1266 in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England.

He died 1347 in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England.

Richard married Isabella PATRIC on Feb 1284 in W …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Sutton於1266年9月29日出生在英國諾廷加姆爾華索普時，他的父親Robert de Sutton27歲，母親Joane Bartram23歲。他於1284年2月5日與Isabella Patrick在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾華索普結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1318年4月25日在他的家鄉享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Sutton, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English surname is also common in Ireland (Wexford, Kildare), where it has been established since the 13th century and Gaelicized as de Sutún.
Jewish (from the former Ottoman Empire, including Syria): unexplained.
English: topographic name from Middle English (bi) suthentune ‘(at the place to the) south of the village’.

variants.name: Setton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Ronit, Yossef.  French Andree, Henri, Jacques, Maryse.
Jewish (from the Ottoman Empire): altered form of   Sutton  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Sutton was born on 29 September 1266, in Warsop, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Robert de Sutton, was 27 and his mother, Joane Bartram, was 23. He married Isabella Patrick on 5 February 1284, in Warsop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 25 April 1318, in his hometown, at the age of 51.

Il a épousé Isabella Patrick le 5 février 1284, à Warsop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 25 avril 1318, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 51 ans.

Februar 1284 in Warsop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Isabella Patrick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 25. ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Sutton(이)가 1266년 9월 29일 영국 노틴그함실르 워소프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Sutton(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane Bartram(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1284년 2월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 워소프에서 Isabella Patrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1318년 4월 25일 그의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Sutton родился 29 сентября 1266 г. в местности варсоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Sutton было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Bartram, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella Patrick 5 февраля 1284 г. в местности варсоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 25 апреля 1318 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 51 год.
